title: B.Sc. (Hons) Forensic Science with specialization in Forensic Physics and Ballistics
content: The B.Sc. (Hons) Forensic Science program with a specialization in Forensic Physics and Ballistics is an undergraduate course designed to provide students with a comprehensive understanding of forensic science principles, with a particular focus on the application of physics in forensic investigations and the science of ballistics. This specialization equips students with the skills and knowledge necessary to analyze physical evidence, investigate firearm-related crimes, and understand the physics behind various forensic processes, preparing them for careers in forensic laboratories, law enforcement agencies, and research institutions. The curriculum combines core forensic science courses with specialized training in forensic physics and ballistics, enhanced by practical training, laboratory work, internships, and field experiences. Graduates of the B.Sc. (Hons) Forensic Science with specialization in Forensic Physics and Ballistics have a wide range of career opportunities, including: Forensic Ballistics Expert, Forensic Physicist, Law Enforcement Officer. Students with a CUET / SAT (DI Code - 71057 ) score will be given preference in the merit list 1) B.Sc (Hons/Hons with Research) in Forensic Science Ist yr. with minimum 50% marks or 5.0 CGPA on a 10 point scale 2) XII with Science with minimum 50% marks. 01 Core Scientific Foundation: Equip students with a strong grounding in physics, material science, and ballistics, integrated with forensic principles for crime scene analysis and evidence interpretation. 02 Specialized Technical Skills: Develop expertise in firearm examination, trajectory analysis, toolmark identification, and advanced instrumentation (e.g., spectroscopy, microscopy, and 3D imaging). 03 Research and Innovation: Cultivate analytical and research skills through hands-on projects, thesis work, and exposure to emerging technologies in forensic physics and ballistics. 04 Legal and Ethical Competence: Train students to present scientific findings effectively in court, adhere to ethical standards, and understand the legal framework governing ballistic evidence. 05 Industry and Career Preparedness: Prepare graduates for careers in law enforcement, forensic labs, defense research, or further studies through internships, case studies, and collaboration with forensic experts. 01 02 03 04 05 06 07 08 01 02 03 01 02 03 Students with a CUET / SAT (DI Code - 71057 ) score will be given preference in the merit list 1) B.Sc (Hons/Hons with Research) in Forensic Science Ist yr. with minimum 50% marks or 5.0 CGPA on a 10 point scale 2) XII with Science with minimum 50% marks.
